title: T-012 — transport_lanes table + RLS org-scoped (§10A.2.1, V-MS-24)
prompt:
# T-012 — transport_lanes table

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §10A.2.1, §11.6 V-MS-24
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add the org-scoped `transport_lanes` master-data table backing Transport Lanes feature (Direction-B closure §10A). Lanes are org master data — NOT site-scoped — per D-MS-4. CHECK (from_site_id != to_site_id) enforced at DB.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0048_transport_lanes.sql` per §10A.2.1 DDL:
   - CREATE TABLE transport_lanes(id, org_id, lane_code, from_site_id FK sites, to_site_id FK sites, mode_of_transport CHECK ('road','rail','air','sea','multimodal'), distance_km NUMERIC(8,2), scheduled_transit_days NUMERIC(5,2), carriers TEXT[], hazmat_allowed bool default false, cold_chain_required bool default false, customs_required bool default false, customs_notes, max_shipment_weight_kg NUMERIC(10,2), special_instructions, notes (max 300 char via app-layer; CHECK length(notes) <= 300 at DB), active bool default true, created_by, created_at, updated_at, CHECK (from_site_id <> to_site_id), UNIQUE (org_id, lane_code)).
   - RLS: org-scoped only (USING org_id = app.current_org_id()) per §10A.2.1 — lanes visible to any user with org context regardless of site_user_access.
   - INDEX idx_lanes_route ON transport_lanes(org_id, from_site_id, to_site_id) WHERE active.
   - INDEX idx_lanes_active ON transport_lanes(org_id) WHERE active.
   - NOTE: transport_lanes is org master data (D-MS-4) — no site_id scoping column here. RLS uses app.current_org_id() only (not app.current_site_id()). D-MS-13 composite (org_id, site_id) index pattern applies to operational site-scoped tables only — this table has from_site_id/to_site_id as FK refs, not as the tenant partitioning column. Composite idx_lanes_org_site not applicable here per D-MS-4.
2. Drizzle schema + zod with mode_of_transport enum, ISO-3 carrier list validation.
3. Tests assert: V-MS-24 CHECK (same from/to rejected) + lane_code unique per org + RLS isolation across orgs + mode_of_transport CHECK + notes ≤ 300 chars.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0048_transport_lanes.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/transport-lanes.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/transport-lanes.zod.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/transport-lanes.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 6→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0048 applied, when \d transport_lanes inspected, then all 19 cols + CHECK(from_site_id<>to_site_id) + UNIQUE(org_id, lane_code) + 2 partial indexes + RLS+FORCE RLS enabled.
2. Given insert with from_site_id == to_site_id, when committed, then DB raises check_violation (V-MS-24).
3. Given org_A has lane_code='LN-001', when second insert with same (org_id='A', lane_code='LN-001'), then DB raises unique_violation.
4. Given two orgs with lanes, when app_user under org_A queries, then only org_A's lanes returned (RLS).
5. Given mode_of_transport='spacecraft', when committed, then DB raises check_violation.
6. Given notes is a 301-char string, when committed, then DB raises check_violation (length ≤ 300).

## Test strategy
- RED: assert V-MS-24 + lane_code unique + RLS isolation + mode CHECK + notes length CHECK before migration applied.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- transport-lanes'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not site-scope this table — lanes are org master data per D-MS-4.
- Do not allow same from/to site — V-MS-24 CHECK at DB layer.
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
- Do not omit the partial index on (active) — lane suggestion query (T-014) needs index-only scan.
- Do not use a domain-validated CHECK for IANA timezone here — that lives on sites table (T-002).
